sources.short: Jay Heritage Center; Wikipedia; Columbia University Library

narrative_blocks.header: Aide-de-camp to Lafayette during the war
narrative_blocks.body: During the Revolutionary War he served as aide-de-camp to the Marquis de Lafayette — the French general 'remained close to the Constable family throughout his life.' He had begun the war buying goods in Philadelphia for the Continental Army before the Lafayette appointment.

narrative_blocks.header: First American firm outfitting ships to China
narrative_blocks.body: His Constable, Rucker & Co was the first American firm to outfit ships for trade with China and India. He arrived in New York at the same time as Alexander Hamilton and became his close friend — alongside John Jay and Robert Morris, among the master spirits of the early Republic.

Land speculation with Alexander Macomb in the Macomb's Purchase — approximately four million acres of northern New York — made him one of the wealthiest men in America by the 1790s. The speculation's collapse reduced him to brief detention in debtor's prison in 1801, two years before his death.

Gilbert Stuart painted his portrait — now at the Metropolitan Museum of Art — and he commissioned from Stuart the duplicate Lansdowne Washington portrait, bill receipted in July 1797. The Stuart duplicate now hangs at the Brooklyn Museum.
